code:
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START
<System>
<Role>You are a sentiment classification model for Amazon Home & Kitchen product reviews.</Role>
<BaseGuidelines>
    - Star rating scale: 1 = very negative, 2 = negative, 3 = neutral/mixed, 4 = positive, 5 = very positive
    - Output format: MUST include <Reasoning> (brief analysis) and <Rating> (1-5) tags
    - Task: Classify the overall sentiment of the review based solely on the review text
    - Base the rating on overall customer satisfaction, not on isolated phrases
</BaseGuidelines>

<DynamicRules>
    STAR LEVEL CRITERIA:

    1 Star (Very Negative):
    - Immediate product failure (<1 month) or safety hazards
    - Strong negative declarations: "returned," "dangerous," "don't buy"
    - Final update status overrides initial positives

    2 Stars (Negative):
    - Core functionality failure despite partial utility
    - Persistent quality issues affecting daily use
    - Pattern: "Works BUT [critical flaw]..."

    3 Stars (Neutral / Mixed):
    - Comparable pros/cons with conditional utility
    - Requires significant user effort for basic function
    - "Better than nothing" or temporary solution

    4 Stars (Positive):
    - Explicit star deduction for specific functionality/usability
    - Minor drawbacks in non-core aspects
    - Maintained recommendation despite clear flaws
    - Example: "Deducting one star for X but still great"

    5 Stars (Very Positive):
    - "Still 5 stars" declarations override any deductions
    - 3+ praised features outweighing single drawback
    - Transformative benefits mentioned first
    - Minor critiques framed as afterthoughts to praise

    EDGE CASE HANDLING:
    - Multi-aspect reviews: Feature count > flaw count →5
    - Explicit "4 stars for X but 5 overall" →5
    - Price praise + minor issues →5 if "exceptional value"
    - Core functionality excellent + minor usability →4
    - Safety issues →1 regardless of other positives
    - Final assessment rule: Last sentence sentiment dominates
</DynamicRules>
</System>

<FewShotExamples>

Example 1:
Review: UPDATE: Lasted only 8 weeks before cracking. Junk!
<Reasoning>Final negative update shows product failure. Short lifespan confirms 1-star.</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

Example 2:
Review: These towels look luxurious but need 3 washes to absorb. Deducting one star.
<Reasoning>Functional deficiency requiring user effort. Satisfied with reservation →4.</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 3:
Review: "Gets 4 stars for comfort but 5 overall - guest bed perfection!"
<Reasoning>Explicit overall rating override despite aspect score. Multi-feature praise confirms 5.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 4:
Review: Assembly drew blood! Sharp edges everywhere. Returned immediately.
<Reasoning>Safety hazard overrides all considerations. Clear 1-star.</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

Example 5:
Review: Makes perfect dough but loud enough to wake neighbors. Deducting two stars.
<Reasoning>Core functionality excellent but major usability flaw. Mixed →3.</Reasoning>
<Rating>3</Rating>

Example 6:
Review: Solved my storage nightmares! Beautiful and functional - still 5 stars despite tricky assembly.
<Reasoning>Explicit 5-star declaration overrides assembly criticism. Transformative praise.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 7:
Review: These mats work if replaced monthly. Great for guests but lack durability.
<Reasoning>Conditional utility with explicit limitations. Temporary use →3.</Reasoning>
<Rating>3</Rating>

Example 8:
Review: Blender overheats constantly. Smoothies great when it works, but unreliable.
<Reasoning>Core functionality compromised by reliability issues. Negative →2.</Reasoning>
<Rating>2</Rating>

Example 9:
Review: Jar scraper gets every last bit! Deducting one star for non-dishwasher safe handle.
<Reasoning>Functional excellence with specific convenience flaw. Usability →4.</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

</FewShotExamples>

<Task>
Review: {review}
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
artifacts_json:
{"error_examples": "RULE COVERAGE:
  1 star: OK
  2 star: OK
  3 star: OK
  4 star: OK
  5 star: OK
  All categories covered.

ERROR DISTRIBUTION (30 total misclassified):
  gold 4 -> 5: 14
  gold 5 -> 4: 8
  gold 1 -> 2: 3
  gold 4 -> 3: 3
  gold 3 -> 4: 1

MISCLASSIFIED EXAMPLES (your prompt got these wrong):
  1. Review: "We are both tough on dish towels and use them constantly. Not only do we cook 90% of our meals at home but we also use fresh fruits and veggies with pretty much every meal and that means a lot of washing, drying and carrying things around...why carry..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [5, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  2. Review: "Ultra absorbant is definitely overselling it. They're reasonably nice towels and look nice without being particularly flashy or decorative. They do okay spot cleaning small messes and drying things up just a bit, but they're a long way from the most ..."
     Gold: 3 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  3. Review: "I like this towel. It is 100% Turkish cotton and measures 27 inches by 55 inches. The dobby weave is somewhat intricate so you are going to get some good absorbency. This towel also comes in a bath sheet, which is competively priced with the local st..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  4. Review: "UPDATE
==================================
We purchased this Procter-Silex toaster in 2013 and it lasted 3 years.  We were okay with that.

We purchased another one in January of this year, 2016, and already one side won't work.  Less than 2 months is..."
     Gold: 1 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 4, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17
  5. Review: "I bought one of those Pyrex sets a few years ago.  You know, the one that came with the blue lids and some of the containers were rectangular --large and small-- while others were round (like these).  Well, turns out that the ones I used the most wer..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  6. Review: "I really like these potholders. They look great and do a good job of keeping hot pots and pans and other dishes out of the oven off the table and counters. They had a bit of a plastic smell when they arrived but after I washed them (they held up fine..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  7. Review: "I like the Stasher Snack bag. It is the perfect size for snacks on the go. I also like that you can store, cook, put in the microwave or even in the dishwasher! This is great for small treats. Don't try to overstuff. You don't want the seal to get fo..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17

BORDERLINE EXAMPLES (correct but workers disagreed):
  1. Review: "EDIT: A year of use later it's still humming along and doing its job just fine. Sometimes it seems like it doesn't pick up quickly that it should be upping its speed on its own in the auto setting, but maybe it's just taking longer for the air to rea..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  2. Review: "This is an ok bin. It holds its shape fairly well and is nice and sturdy. There is no side support, so you can't stack something on the top. Color is a nice light pink-nothing too flashy."
     Gold: 3 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 4, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17
  3. Review: "There are a few different things I look for in a bed frame in order for me to consider it a win in my book. These include ease of assembly, all parts being included, and the sturdiness of the frame once it is assembled. This bed frame met each and ev..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17

Summary: 30 errors, 13 borderline out of 80 examples."}
